data.title: São Paulo, Brazil, will launch a pilot project for blockchain-based microloans for farmers.
data.description: São Paulo, Brazil's economic center, has reportedly announced a pilot project to provide microloans to farmers using blockchain technology. The initiative aims to improve the efficiency and transparency of credit allocation while reducing administrative costs, thereby helping agricultural producers, particularly small and medium-sized farmers, access financial support more easily. This move marks a further exploration by Brazilian local governments in applying Web3 technology to the real economy and improving the agricultural financing environment. (CoinDesk)

data.title: A whale deposited $13.12 million USDC into Hyperliquid to hoard ENA.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale address deposited $13.12 million USDC into Hyperliquid over the past four days to accumulate ENA on the spot market. As of now, this whale has spent $7.25 million to buy 25.56 million ENA, and still holds $5.78 million USDC in its account, with the buying activity continuing.

data.title: BlackRock Receives Large Crypto Transfers from Coinbase
data.description:
BlackRock receives 300 BTC and 16,629 ETH from Coinbase, marking significant institutional inflow in crypto management.
source: https://coincu.com/news/blackrock-coinbase-crypto-transfers/

data.title: UK orders crypto platforms to log all user transactions starting 2026
data.description:
UK crypto platforms will be required to report all user transactions from 2026.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/uk-crypto-platforms-to-log-all-transactions/

data.title: U.S. Bank Tests Custom Stablecoin on Stellar Network
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. Bank, in collaboration with PwC and the Stellar Development Foundation (SDF), has announced the testing of a custom stablecoin issuance on the Stellar network. This initiative marks a significant step in exploring blockchain technology for financial services. The partnership aims to leverage Stellar's capabilities to enhance the efficiency and security of digital transactions.

data.title: A whale deposited 500 BTC into Binance and is expected to lose approximately $10.8 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale address deposited 500 BTC (approximately $45.61 million) into Binance after holding the assets for 1.5 months. This whale had previously withdrawn these funds from Binance when they were worth approximately $55.79 million; selling them after this deposit would result in a loss of approximately $10.8 million.

data.title: Planet Morning News | November 29
data.description:
1. CEA Industries (BNC) announces new board members.
2. Yala: All native BTC will be removed from the protocol under institutional model; future focus will shift to AI-driven agent prediction.
3. CME data center outage suspends futures and options trading, affecting trillions of dollars in contracts.
4. Turkmenistan passes cryptocurrency regulatory law, to be implemented in 2026 and subject to strict state control.
5. Tether suspends Bitcoin mining in Uruguay due to rising energy costs.
6. Opinion: Key bull market signal; ERC-20 stablecoin supply remains at a record $185 billion.
7. All CME markets are now open and trading has resumed.
8. Circle issues 500 million USDC on Solana.
9. Opinion: XRP technical charts and on-chain data suggest a short-term rebound to $2.80.
10. Binance testnet API update adds Chinese support; potentially influenced by this news, meme token Binance Life rises over 30% in a short period.
11. Tom Lee's Bitmine buys another 20,532 tokens. 12. OpenSea CMO: $150 million public offering news is fake news. 13. BlackRock received $78.15 million worth of BTC and ETH from Coinbase in the past 10 minutes. 14. CoinShares withdraws its SEC application to stake Solana ETFs. 15. UK government to implement new crypto tax rules from January 2026, cracking down on tax evasion. 16. Arthur Hayes: Bitcoin has bottomed out, maintains prediction of reaching $250,000 by the end of the year.

data.title: Memecoin Activity Collapses on Solana, Hitting Lowest Share Since 2023 Boom
data.description:
Solana’s on-chain landscape is shifting again, and this time, memecoins are losing ground. New data shows that meme-related trading now makes up less than 5% of Solana’s daily DEX volume, marking the lowest share since the explosive memecoin cycle that began two years ago.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/memecoin-activity-collapses-on-solana-hitting-lowest-share-since-2023-boom/

data.title: Belarus Prepares for FATF Evaluation with Cryptocurrency Wallet Registry
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Belarus has established and is updating a registry dedicated to recording cryptocurrency wallets associated with criminals, in preparation for an evaluation by the Financial Action Task Force (FATF), which is akin to the regional anti-money laundering organization EAG. The head of Belarus's main auditing body highlighted that the country has also enacted regulations concerning the taxation of cryptocurrency transactions, along with other measures to ensure transparency in the digital asset sector.

data.description: On Nov 29, 2025, 01:15 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 91,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 91,038.101563 USDT, with a narrowed 0.16% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: Bitcoin has bottomed out; I maintain my prediction of reaching $250,000 by the end of the year.
data.description: BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes recently stated on a program that despite only about 30 days left until the end of the year, he maintains his prediction that Bitcoin will rise to $250,000. Hayes believes that Bitcoin's previous pullback to $80,600 and the bottoming out of dollar liquidity have both contributed to this. He points out that with the deleveraging in October and the end of institutional "basis trading" strategies through ETFs, the market has completed its reset. Combined with macroeconomic expectations that the Federal Reserve may end quantitative tightening (QT) and further cut interest rates, these catalysts are expected to push Bitcoin past its current all-time high of $126,220, reaching the $250,000 target before the end of the year. (The Crypto Basic)

data.title: CoinShares withdraws its SEC filing regarding staking Solana ETFs.
data.description: Asset management firm CoinShares officially withdrew its application to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Friday for a staked Solana ETF. The filing shows that the registration statement was intended to register shares related to a ultimately unsuccessful transaction; no shares were actually sold, and no future sales will be made under the statement. Previously, REX-Osprey and Bitwise launched staked SOL ETFs in the U.S. in June and October, respectively. Although these ETFs attracted over $369 million in inflows in November, the price of SOL has recently performed poorly, hitting a five-month low of around $120. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: CoinShares Withdraws Solana ETF Application from SEC
data.description: According to Odaily, asset management firm CoinShares has officially withdrawn its application for a Solana staking ETF from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) as of Friday. The filing indicated that the registration statement was initially intended to register shares related to a transaction that ultimately did not take effect, and no shares were sold under this statement, nor will they be sold in the future. Previously, staking SOL ETFs launched by REX-Osprey and Bitwise were listed in the U.S. in June and October of this year, respectively. Despite these related ETF products attracting over $369 million in inflows in November, the price of SOL has recently been underperforming, reaching a five-month low of approximately $120.

data.title: The UK government will implement new crypto tax rules from January 2026 to crack down on tax evasion.
data.description: The UK's HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) has released new guidance announcing new rules aimed at combating cryptocurrency tax evasion, effective January 1, 2026. According to the guidance, cryptocurrency exchanges operating in the UK must begin collecting detailed transaction records and complete information from all UK customers. HMRC will use the collected data to cross-check users' tax returns to ensure tax compliance, and violators will face sanctions. Furthermore, the UK's new guidelines align with the OECD's crypto-asset reporting framework, aiming to increase transparency in the digital asset market. (CoinDesk)
